title: California Approves $1.4 billion Plan to Build Thousands More Vehicle Chargers, Boost ZEV Infrastructure
content: The California Energy Commission (CEC) today approved a $1.4 billion investment plan that accelerates progress on the stateâ€™s EV charging and hydrogen refueling goals. These investments will help deploy infrastructure for light, medium, and heavy-duty zero-emission vehicles (ZEV) across California, expanding the most extensive charging and hydrogen refueling network in the country.
author: Analysts Account
